content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1988


Honorable John Heilman
Councilmember, City of West Hollywood
8611 Santa Monica Blvd.
West Hollywood, CA  90069-4109

Re:  Your Requests for Advice
     Our File Nos.  A-88-260
                    A-88-266

Dear Mr. Heilman:

     Your letters requesting advice about application of Section
85400 of the Political Reform Act, as added by Proposition 73,
were received on July 7 and 12, 1988. If you have any
questions about your advice requests, you may contact Margarita
Altamirano, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at
• (916) 322-5901.

     Section 85400 limits to $1,000 in a calendar year
acceptance by an elected official of gifts or honoraria
received or concerning the governmental process. As you know,
Section 85400 becomes operative on January 1, 1989. Therefore,
presently Section 85400 does not apply to gifts and honoraria
received by an elected official.

     Because of the delayed operative date of most of the
provisions of the recently enacted campaign finance reform
initiatives and the number of questions regarding
implementation, we are attempting to answer questions in an
organized and efficient manner. We expect to answer your
request in a timely manner, but not within the usual twenty-one
working days. (Section 83114(b).) Furthermore, because both
your letters request advice about the same section of
Proposition 73, we will answer both requests in one letter.

     If you have any questions or would like to discuss your
request, please call Ms. Altamirano. As Ms. Altamirano has


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Honorable John Heilman
July 18, 1988
Page -2-


explained to you, you should be aware that your letters and our
response are public records which may be disclosed to the
public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Gregory W. Baughier
Executive Director

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 18, 1989

Honorable John Heilman
Councilmember
City of West Hollywood
8611 Santa Monica Blvd.
West Hollywood, CA 90069-4109

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-260

Dear Councilmember Heilman:

Please excuse my delay in responding to your request for
advice regarding application of the provisions of Section
85300 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"), as amended by
Proposition 73, relative to the limitations imposed on gifts
and honoraria.

As you are aware, the Commission has been attempting to
address the numerous concerns regarding implementation of
Proposition 73 through regulations and formal opinions. At
its January meeting, the Commission was asked to approve
formal notice of proposed Regulation 18540 (copy enclosed).
The proposed regulation clarifies the application of the
limitation on gifts and honoraria. We anticipate the
proposed regulation will be formally noticed for adoption in
March.

I have enclosed a copy of the staff's discussion
memorandum, along with a copy of the proposed regulation, for
your information. You will note that staff is recommending
that the limitation not apply to situations where payments
received by elected officials are "income" rather than "gifts
or honoraria." Included within the definition of income, for
purposes of the regulation, are payments received from a bona
fide educational or academic institution for classroom
teaching (subdivision (c)(2)), and payments received as a
consequence of a contract with a publishing house for
publication of a book or article (subdivision (c)(3)).

We are currently soliciting comments on the proposed
regulation. If approved as noticed, public officials whose
profession, outside of public office, is teaching or writing
will be able to continue receiving income for such work.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Honorable John Heilman
January 18, 1989
Page 2

Any comments you would like to submit regarding the
proposed regulation should be directed to the Legal Division,
Fair Political Practices Commission, 428 J Street, Suite 800,
Sacramento, California 95814.

If you have any questions regarding this response to
your inquiry, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh

Enclosure

City of
West Hollywood

JU 12 C UU JN JO

City Council
Hom, Albert
Mayor
Abbe Land
Mayor Pro Tempore
John Heilman
Stephen Schulte
Paul Koretz

July 5, 1988

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
Mr. Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Griffiths and Mr. Leidigh:

In addition to my general concerns regarding
Proposition 73's applicability to people who are employed
writing or teaching, I have a specific question. Because I
am still negotiating the details of this project, I would
appreciate it if you would maintain confidentiality regarding
this matter.

As you know, the incorporation and development of our
city attracted a great deal of international and national
attention. I have developed a proposal to write a book about
our experience in incorporating the city and in struggling
through our first several years of cityhood. The proposal
has been met with interest, and I am proceeding to refine and
further develop my ideas on this project. Should my proposal
be accepted and the book come to fruition, I am curious as to
whether Proposition 73 would apply to me. I have serious
doubts as to whether any legitimate purpose could be served
by applying the provisions of Proposition 73 to my situation,
and I also believe that any application in this regard would
raise serious first amendment questions. Your analysis and
advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you once again for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

John Heilman
John Heilman
Councilmember

JH:bc

[illegible] Sayre Manor Boulevard, West Hollywood, CA 90069-1[illegible]

City of
West Hollywood

JUL 7 8 05 AM 88

City Council
Helen Albert
Mayor
Abby Seixas
Mayor Pro Tempore
John Heilman
Stephen Schulte
Paul Koretz

June 30, 1988

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
Mr. Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804-0807

Dear Ms. Griffiths and Mr. Leidigh:

     I am seeking advice from you regarding the provisions
of Proposition 73. I know that you have been deluged with
requests regarding the provision which prohibits bulk
mailings by government agencies. My request, however, has to
do with the provision which prohibits elected officeholders
from accepting any gift or honorarium for any speech, article
or published work on a subject relating to the governmental
process from any single source which is in excess of $1,000
in any calendar year. As you are aware, many local elected
officials receive only a small salary for being
councilmembers. Those of us who are not independently
wealthy have to work for a living. Some of us make our
living by writing and teaching. For example, I annually
prepare a book dealing with decisions by the Supreme Court.
I am paid over $1,000 for that work from a single source.
What impact will Proposition 73 have on my livelihood?

     I also am aware that a number of local elected
officials teach classes at colleges and universities
regarding the governmental process. Will the provisions of
Proposition 73 have any impact on those elected officials who
draw a supplemental salary from an educational institution
for teaching about the governmental process?

     I certainly understand the intent of Proposition 73 and
am supportive of some of what the authors were trying to
prohibit. My reading of the language, however, indicates

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
Mr. Robert E. Leidigh
June 30, 1988
Page Two


that there are a number of uncertainties created by the
initiative. Please provide me with your analysis at your
earliest convenience. Thank you for all your hard work and
good luck in unraveling the questions created by Proposition
73.

Very truly yours,


[illegible signature]

John Heilman
Councilmember

JH:bc

P.S. I am requesting confidential advice on a specific
matter under separate cover.

cc: Jim Petzke, Supervisor Edmund Edelman's Office
Fred Woocher, The Center for Law in the Public
Interest
Ramona Ripston, ACLU

88 260

City of
West Hollywood

JUL 12 8 00 AM '88

City Council

Helen Albert
Mayor
Abbe Land
Mayor Pro Tempore
John Heilman
Stephen Schulte
Paul Koretz

July 5, 1988


Ms. Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
Mr. Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804-0807

Dear Ms. Griffiths and Mr. Leidigh:

     In addition to my general concerns regarding
Proposition 73's applicability to people who are employed
writing or teaching, I have a specific question. Because I
am still negotiating the details of this project, it would
appreciate it if you would maintain confidentiality regarding
this matter.

     As you know, the incorporation and development of our
city attracted a great deal of international and national
attention. I have developed a proposal to write a book about
our experience in incorporating the city and in struggling
through our first several years of cityhood. The proposal
has been met with interest, and I am proceeding to refine and
further develop my ideas on this project. Should my proposal
be accepted and the book come to fruition, I am curious as to
whether Proposition 73 would apply to me. I have serious
doubts as to whether any legitimate purpose could be served
by applying the provisions of Proposition 73 to my situation,
and I also believe that any application in this regard would
raise serious first amendment questions. Your analysis and
advice would be greatly appreciated.

     Thank you once again for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

John Heilman
John Heilman
Councilmember
